name: Partial v-ATPase perturbation activated a lysosomal surveillance response and extended worm healthspan
summary: A 2025 C. elegans study identifies an adaptive lysosomal surveillance response that improves proteotoxic-stress handling and longevity phenotypes.
statement: In C. elegans, partial RNAi of v-ATPase subunits such as vha-6 induced a lysosomal surveillance response that boosted lysosomal activity, improved protein-aggregate clearance, and extended healthspan.
population_or_model: C. elegans partial v-ATPase RNAi and proteotoxic disease models
time_horizon: Adult worm lifespan and aggregate-clearance assays
quantitative_note: The abstract and institutional summary describe lifespan extension of about 60% after silencing selected v-ATPase subunits.
caveats: This is worm evidence and depends on a stress-response induction paradigm.
v-ATPase biology is context-sensitive, and partial deacidification should not be read as a direct mammalian therapeutic recommendation.
The result supports a targetable lysosomal response more than a ready intervention.
